definition: The user-facing knowledge object — a bounded, readable unit that presents institutional knowledge to stewards and readers.
The internal reasoning atom — a discrete assertion supported by evidence, used to build and validate Cards.
The relational grammar connecting Cards, Claims, and institutional objects — replaces folder logic with explicit verbs.
A repeatable transformation object that takes named inputs through an operating rule to produce bounded outputs.
Distinct from Engine — guides human attention rather than mechanically transforming material.
Raw institutional memory — becoming events, revisions, failed ideas, abandoned paths, and contradictions without forced narrative.
Curated wisdom and steward notes — distinct from the raw Archive of Becoming.
Knowledge that has passed through admission, review, and ratification gates — not everything published or displayed.
Material under active review, revision, or practice — not yet locked, not yet abandoned.
Material that has passed ratification gates — constitutional, charter, or specification locks apply.
The trace of where material came from, who stewarded it, and what gates it passed — essential for archive integrity.
The process by which an Engine transforms source material into a Card ecosystem — demonstrated by Stewardship Exercise 001.
Implementation infrastructure — software, deployment, APIs — that serves the institution without governing it.
Reader-facing or visual material that explains and accompanies governing doctrine — not itself governing authority.
